chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, LARKANA C.P.No.D-1012 of 2025 Present: Mr. Justice Adnan Iqbal Chaudhry Mr. Justice Abdul Hamid Bhurgri Petitioners: Zamir Hussain and another through M/s. Muhammad Ashique Dhamraho and Jam Zuhaib Ahmed Kamario, Advocates Respondents: Federation of Pakistan Mr. Riaz Hussain Khoso, D.A.G Mr. Habibullah G. Ghouri, Advocate for Respondents No.2 to 4. Date of Hearing: 03.03.2026 Date of Order: 03.03.2026 O R D E R Adnan Iqbal Chaudhry J. - The petitioners pray for a direction to the SSGC to replace disconnected gas meters and restore gas connection to the petitioners’ premises. As per comments of the SSGC, gas was disconnected as the petitioners were using a domestic connection for commercial purposes and had also tampered the meter; that upon detection of unauthorized use of gas, the SSGC has raised a bill/claim upon the petitioners, which has yet to be settled. Though learned counsel for the petitioners deny the allegations, the petitioners have not filed any rejoinder to the comments of the SSGC. 2. Apparently, the dispute between petitioners and SSGC is covered by the provisions of The Gas (Theft Control and Recovery) Act, 2016. Under sections 6 of said Act, a remedy is available to the petitioner before the Gas Utility Court, which enjoys exclusive jurisdiction under section 4 of said Act. Since an alternate remedy is available, the petition is not maintainable. Same is dismissed. Judge Judge M Yousuf Panhwar/**
